label: false-dup
a: Every database migration must include a down() method that reverses the migration. Migrations without a rollback path are not permitted.
b: Migrations run sequentially in the order of their timestamp prefix. Parallel migration execution is not permitted — concurrent schema changes can cause deadlocks and unpredictable results.
reviewer_notes: Both rules about migrations but different concerns: A is reversibility (down()); B is ordering/concurrency (sequential, no parallel).
